name: Raul & Rosy School Bus LLC
recent_news: No public M&A or operational news found as of 2026.
fit_notes: Micro-operator (~28 employees, ~$1.2M rev) in dense Miami-Dade private/charter school market. Likely too small for standalone tuck-in but could be route-acquisition target in a Florida roll-up; ownership stability and contracts unverifiable.

name: Skyliner Transportation Bus
districts_or_customers: Doral / Miami-Dade area (likely private/charter school routes)
recent_news: No public M&A or operational news found as of 2026.
fit_notes: Single-tier Doral operator (~28 employees, ~$1.2M rev). Likely Hispanic/family-owned private school transport; useful only as a small Miami-Dade route bolt-on, not a standalone platform tuck-in.

name: Auto Bus Planet Inc
districts_or_customers: Likely NYC DOE / OPT yellow-bus subcontract or charter routes (Queens-based)
recent_news: No public M&A or operational news found as of 2026.
fit_notes: Small Queens-based operator (~31 employees, ~$1.2M rev). NYC yellow-bus market is heavily consolidated (NYCSBUS, Logan, Reliant); micro-vendors typically subcontract. Limited standalone tuck-in value; possibly a route/medallion play.

name: BCDPF Greenwood Bus Ctr LLC
districts_or_customers: Greenwood, IN area (Johnson County); possible Greenwood Community Schools contractor or charter operator
recent_news: No public M&A or operational news found as of 2026. Name (BCDPF) suggests a holding LLC, possibly a maintenance/depot facility rather than a primary contractor.
fit_notes: Unclear whether operating contractor vs. a real-estate/depot LLC for charter buses. Indianapolis-metro location is attractive for Midwest expansion, but unverified ownership and missing executive contact lower priority until further diligence.

name: Magic School Bus TRNSPRTN LLC
districts_or_customers: Naples / Collier County area; likely private school or charter routes
recent_news: Recently formed entity (filed ~2025 per source year); no M&A or operational news found.
fit_notes: Newly formed Naples micro-operator (~28 employees, ~$1.2M rev). Likely too early-stage and small for tuck-in; trademark concerns with Scholastic 'Magic School Bus' brand. Monitor but deprioritize.
